title: 2026 DSE Speaking 口試題目合集｜即時更新 (10/3 - 19/3)
seoTitle: 2026 DSE Speaking 口試題目合集｜即時更新 (10/3 - 22/3)
seoDescription: 2026 DSE Speaking 實戰題目分享平台｜全港考生匯報最新口試真題、Group Discussion同Individual Response！3月10-22日最新更新，新增題目，刻不容緩把握先機，考試趨勢一手掌握！
author: JTV
content:
未輪到你，或者就嚟輪到你考 DSE Speaking/Oral ? dse.best 為學生整立咗，有由全港考生親身匯報嘅題目，涵蓋 Group Interaction + Individual Response question sets!

呢個專頁由我哋團隊每日主動更新與維護，確保你隨時掌握最新趨勢，考前溫習立竿見影！無論分享定借鑒，都希望可以幫到大家一齊衝 5** ❗

⚠️ 重要聲明

本頁純粹係aggregate考生網上d posts (IG, threads)，匯集考生自願分享嘅題目，無法保證內容完全準確或與你考場相同，亦有可能出現不實資料。所有資訊僅供參考，不代表官方立場，與HKEAA完全無關。請以考試官方指引為準，謹慎使用。

* = unconfirmed or inferred

Day 1 (10/3 Tue)

10/3 5:00 PM

1.1 Two Dish Rice

Part A Group Interaction

  • why are "two-dish rice" meals so common in Hong Kong

  • what are the advantages and disadvantages of eating two-dish rice

  • how can we promote "two-dish rice" to tourists

  • anything else you think is important

 

Part B Individual Response

  1. How does social media influence people's eating habits?

  2. How can two-dish rice improve its service?

  3. Which one is more important, food quality or price?

  4. Do you agree that two-dish rice will become a culture in HK?

  5. What do you eat for lunch?

  6. Have you ever eaten two-dish rice?

  7. How would you recommend two-dish rice to others?

  8. Explain whether economic conditions could potentially affect people's choices of meals.

  9. How do social media influencers promote two-dish rice?

10/3 6:00

1.2 Importance of Financial Literacy among teenagers

Part A Group Interaction

  • what is the importance of being financially literate

  • what strategies can students adopt to save money

  • how can we promote financial education in schools

  • anything else you think is important

 

Part B Individual Response

  1. Why do people think managing money is difficult?

  2. If you were given a thousand dollars, how much will you save?

  3. Why don't people nowadays have a habit of saving money?

  4. Do you think you buy more things than you need?

  5. Why is it difficult for some people to save money?

  6. How does peer pressure influence teenagers in financial decisions?

  7. Is having an emergency fund important for you and your family?

  8. Why are there some people with weaker financial concepts?

10/3 7:00

1.3 Online Dishonesty

Part A Group Interaction

  • reason of people telling lies online

  • how you feel when your friend lied online

  • how to protect from lies online

  • anything else you think is important

 

Part B Individual Response

  • Do you often use social media?

  • Is it important to be honest online?

  • Would you like to add photos to your posts on social media?

  • Have you ever seen people being dishonest online?

  • Do you agree with people posting edited photos?

  • Is it common for people to exaggerate their achievements online?

  • Do you think it is dishonest to post text online that is written by AI?

  • How do you make sure the information online is true?

Day 2 (11/3 Wed)

11/3 5:00

2.1 Popular Food and Drinks

Part A Group Interaction

  • why are some food and drinks popular on social media

  • how can social media influence food and drink options

  • what are the opportunities and challenges the matcha industry may face due to social media

  • anything else yоu think is important

 

Part B Individual Response

  1. Do you like matcha?

  2. What are your favourite food or drink?

  3. Why has matcha become more popular nowadays?

  4. Would you like to try different matcha foods or drinks?

  5. Do you want to learn a traditional craft or skill?

  6. Will matcha replace other soft drinks?

  7. How can we promote traditional food in society?

  8. Do you think traditional food culture, such as matcha, should be protected?

11/3 6:00

2.2 E-commerce

Part A Group Interaction

  • advantages and disadvantages of e-commerce on consumers and retailers

  • strategies for physical stores to survive

  • what government can do to help the local retailers

  • anything else you think is important

 

Part B Individual Response

  1. Do you like shopping online?

  2. Do you think online shopping can be addictive?

  3. Which one do you prefer, online shopping or physical shopping?

  4. Why do some people still choose physical stores?

  5. What can consumers do to support physical shops?

  6. Do delivery services make online shopping more attractive?

  7. Is money the most important factor affecting the choice of consumers?

  8. How does the closure of physical stores affect the local community?

11/3 7:00

2.3 Movie Industry

Part A Group Interaction

Background: Your class is going to present few Hong Kong films to visiting international students.

  • what do people like about Hong Kong films

  • what types of film should be shared to international students

  • challenges that international students may have when watching the film

  • anything else you think is important

 

Part B Individual Response

  1. Do you like watching movies?

  2. What is your favourite movie?

  3. Do boys and girls like the same type of movie?

  4. Do you want to work in the film industry?

  5. Do you enjoy watching movies from foreign countries?

  6. Do you prefer in-real-life films or fantasy films?

  7. Will a surprising ending make a movie enjoyable?

  8. Do you think a famous star is needed for a successful mоvie?

Day 3 (12/3 Thu)

12/3 5:00

3.1 Robotaxi

Part A Group Interaction

  • what are the benefits of introducing robotaxi in Hong Kong

  • what are the disadvantages of introducing robotaxi in Hong Kong

  • can self-driving technоlogies be implemented in other forms of transportation

  • anything else you think is important

 

Part B Individual Response

  1. Would you travel by taxi?

  2. Do you think riding a taxi is expensive?

  3. Do you prefer to ride a taxi, or a bus?

  4. Would you try to ride a robotaxi?

  5. In what occasion would you choose to take a taxi rather than public transport?

  6. Do you think robotaxis can reduce traffic congestion?

  7. How can taxi services be improved in Hong Kong?

  8. Do you think robotaxis will one day replace human drivers?

12/3 6:00

3.2 Tipping Culture

Part A Group Interaction

  • what jobs deserve tips

  • will tipping lead to better services

  • what are the possible problems if tipping culture spreads to Hong Kong

  • anything else you think is important

 

Part B Individual Response

  1. Have you tipped before?

  2. Is tipping common in Hong Kong?

  3. Would you choose a job that receives tips?

  4. How much should we tip a delivery driver?

  5. Should tips still be given when the service is bad?

  6. Should tipping be voluntary or compulsory?

  7. Why do some restaurants adopt a no-tipping system?

  8. Should tips for waiters be shared with the others in the restaurant?

12/3 7:00

3.3 Home Remedies

Part A Group Interaction

  • what are the advantages of home remedies

  • what are the possible risks of using home remedies

  • are home remedies popular in Hong Kong

  • anything else you think is important

 

Part B Individual Response

  1. Dо you like drinking green tea?

  2. Do you trust home remedies?

  3. Have you and your family tried home remedies before?

  4. Do you prefer to see a doctor or to use home remedies?

  5. Do you think home remedies are safer than common medicine?

  6. Why do some people avoid medical assistance?

  7. Why do so many people prefer home remedies rather than medicine?

  8. Tо what extent will home remedies replace medicine?

Day 4 (13/3 Thu)

13/3 5:00

4.1 Start-up for teenagers

Part A Group Interaction

  • which business would you choose

  • what are the benefits and challenges of running such businesses in school

  • how can parents and school support the business

  • anything else you think is important

 

Part B Individual Response

  1. Do you want to start a business in the future?

  2. Is Hong Kong a good place for students to start a business?

  3. Do you think young people can run a business successfully?

  4. What factors would you like to consider when you start up a business?

  5. Why are more and more teenagers running their businesses nowadays?

  6. Should students only focus on academic studies or develop business skills also?

  7. Which would you choose, handicraft or service industry?

  8. Which kind of business is more challenging, providing service or handmaking products?

13/3 6:00

4.2 AI in workplace

Part A Group Interaction

  • what jobs will be replaced by AI

  • are there tasks that AI cannot do successfully

  • what are the disadvantages if tasks are done by AI

  • anything else you think is important

 

Part B Individual Response

  1. Do you love cooking?

  2. Do you like to work with AI robots?

  3. Do you think AI will cause unemployment?

  4. Would you like to try food cooked by AI robots?

  5. Would you like to be served by robots in restaurants?

  6. Should AI be used to write work letters?

  7. If you have an AI service bot, what do you want it to do?

  8. Will you make use of AI when you are pursuing your future career?

13/3 7:00

4.3 Choosing a Major

Part A Group Interaction

  • what should students consider when choosing a major in higher education

  • why are some programmes popular

  • how do parents influence students' preferences

  • anything else you think is important

 

Part B Individual Response

  1. Would you like to receive higher education?

  2. Do you prefer receiving higher education locally or overseas?

  3. Is money a factor for you to choose a major?

  4. What is the biggest challenge when choosing a major?

  5. What can students do to adapt to the job market?

  6. How can schools help students to adapt to the job market?

  7. Does studying at top universities guarantee career success?

  8. Which one is more important when choosing a major, interest or career prospect?

Anxious? Try our Individual Response Practice Tool!
